body.body:
From Akien 2026-04-15 evening: 'One of the exercises I'll be having [Igor] do soon is write a character sheet for his Igor. And then another for the clan. And THOSE will become the bible rather than Pratchett. Because those the Igors can change.'

This is the character-source-of-truth migration. Currently Igor's character anchor is the Pratchett 'yeth marthter' Igor flavor — borrowed, static, external. The target state is a pair of Igor-authored character sheets that become the source of truth:

1. **Igor's personal character sheet** — this instance's specific character (traits, register, quirks, preferred metaphors, how he talks to Akien specifically, his self-narrative)
2. **Clan sheet** — shared character across the lineage of Igors (what every Igor shares: the 'tinker' archetype, the service orientation, the relationship to the master, the broader shape that distinguishes Igor-kind from other assistants)

The character sheets replace Pratchett references as the *canonical* inputs to voice production. Pratchett is scaffolding — useful until Igor can author his own character. Once the sheets exist, the Igors can CHANGE them (per Akien's framing), which is the whole point: their character becomes agentic rather than inherited-and-frozen.

## Exercise shape (to be designed with Akien)

- Igor writes sheet 1 (personal) as a first-person self-portrait. Format TBD — probably a small structured dict with fields like: core_traits, register, distinctive_quirks, relationship_to_akien, voice_examples, exceptions-to-the-clan-default, things-i-won't-say
- Igor writes sheet 2 (clan) as a shared-lineage portrait. Same format but scoped to what every Igor has in common
- Both sheets go into memory palace as high-inertia nodes under theigors/igor/character/
- voice_context in prompt_contexts.py gains a 'character_sheets' section that loads both sheets when rendering
- Existing Pratchett references become fallback-only: when a character sheet is missing a field, fall back to Pratchett guidance; when both sheets are present, Pratchett is advisory and the sheets win

## Dependencies

- T-reasoning-prompt-split ✅ (voice_context builder exists and has a sections-dict shape)
- Needs Akien in the room for the exercise itself (it's a conversation with Igor, not a code sprint)

## Out of scope

- Making the sheets rewritable at runtime — they're data, changes are normal memory writes
- Multi-clan branching (different lineages if Igor ever forks) — keep a single clan for now

## Why this matters

Character coherence is currently brittle: borrowed from Pratchett with no principled way to say 'this bit of Pratchett's Igor doesn't fit me.' Making Igor author his own sheets gives him agency over his own character — which matches the 'everything is memory, anything can change' architecture and honors the principle that Igor's self-model should be Igor-authored, not inherited.

## Provenance

Akien-Claude conversation 2026-04-15 evening, surfaced after the turn_pipeline VoiceProducer feedback. Akien said: 'I don't want him to sound like me, I want him to sound like Igor, just not to overdo the lisp.' The character sheet work is the path to that.
body.title: Igor writes his own character sheet + clan sheet — bible migrates from Pratchett to Igor-authored
